conversionTrackingId.description: You will find this information in the event snippet for your conversion action, for example `send_to: AW-CONVERSION_ID/AW-CONVERSION_LABEL`. In the sample snippet, AW-CONVERSION_ID stands for the conversion ID unique to your account. Enter the conversion ID, without the AW- prefix. **Required if you are using a mapping that sends data to the legacy Google Enhanced Conversions API (i.e. Upload Enhanced Conversion (Legacy) Action).**
conversionTrackingId.label: Conversion ID

customerId.description: ID of your Google Ads Account. This should be 10-digits and in XXX-XXX-XXXX format. **Required if you are using a mapping that sends data to the Google Ads API.**
customerId.label: Customer ID

loginCustomerId.description: ID of your Google Ads Manager Account. This should be 10-digits and in XXX-XXX-XXXX format. **Required if you want your Manager Account to handle data for its connected accounts.**
loginCustomerId.label: Login Customer ID

supports_conversions.description: Mark true if you are using uploadCallConversion, uploadClickConversion or uploadConversionAdjustment. If you plan to use userLists alone or in combination with the others, mark as false.
supports_conversions.label: Supports Conversions

external_id_type.description: Customer match upload key types. Required if you are using UserLists. Not used by the other actions.
external_id_type.label: External ID Type

app_id.description: A string that uniquely identifies a mobile application from which the data was collected. Required if external ID type is mobile advertising ID
app_id.label: App ID

oauth.description: Authorizes Segment to OAuth to the Destination API
oauth.label: OAuth

access-token.description: The (legacy) access token provided by Destination API after the OAuth handshake.

access_token.description: The access token provided by Destination API after the OAuth handshake.

appId.description: The App ID, retrieved via Destination API post-auth.

appName.description: The authorized user App, retrieved via Destination API on settings view load.

createdBy.description: Email address of segment user who connected OAuth.

displayName.description: The authorized user, retrieved via Destination API on settings view load.

refresh_token.description: The refresh token provided by Destination API after the OAuth handshake.

__segment_internal_engage_batch_sync.label: Indicates that this is a batched (or synced) destination on some schedule
__segment_internal_engage_batch_sync.description: Indicates that this is a batched (or synced) destination on some schedule

actions.name: Call Conversion V2
actions.description: Send an offline call conversion to the Google Ads API.

fields.label: Conversion Action ID
fields.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

conversion_action.title: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

fields.label: Caller ID
fields.description: The caller ID from which this call was placed. Caller ID is expected to be in E.164 format with preceding + sign, e.g. "+16502531234".

caller_id.title: Caller ID
caller_id.description: The caller ID from which this call was placed. Caller ID is expected to be in E.164 format with preceding + sign, e.g. "+16502531234".

fields.label: Call Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the call occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

call_timestamp.title: Call Timestamp
call_timestamp.description: The date time at which the call occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

fields.label: Conversion Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

conversion_timestamp.title: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

fields.label: Value
fields.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

fields.label: Currency
fields.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

fields.label: Custom Variables
fields.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables).

custom_variables.title: Custom Variables
custom_variables.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables).

fields.label: Ad User Data Consent State
fields.description: This represents consent for ad user data. For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_user_data_consent_state.title: Ad User Data Consent State
ad_user_data_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad user data. For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

fields.label: Ad Personalization Consent State
fields.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_personalization_consent_state.title: Ad Personalization Consent State
ad_personalization_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

fields.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
fields.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

enable_batching.title: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

fields.label: Batch Size
fields.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

batch_size.title: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

fields.label: How to sync records
fields.description: Define how the records from your destination will be synced.

actions.name: Click Conversion V2
actions.description: Send an offline click conversion to the Google Ads API.

fields.label: Conversion Action ID
fields.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

conversion_action.title: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

fields.label: GCLID
fields.description: The Google click ID (gclid) associated with this conversion.

gclid.title: GCLID
gclid.description: The Google click ID (gclid) associated with this conversion.

fields.label: GBRAID
fields.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with app conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

gbraid.title: GBRAID
gbraid.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with app conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

fields.label: WBRAID
fields.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with web conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

wbraid.title: WBRAID
wbraid.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with web conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

fields.label: User IP Address
fields.description: The IP address of the user who initiated the conversion.

user_ip_address.title: User IP Address
user_ip_address.description: The IP address of the user who initiated the conversion.

fields.label: Session Attributes (Encoded)
fields.description: A base64url-encoded JSON string containing session attributes collected from the user's browser. Provides additional attribution context if gclid, gbraid, or user identifiers are missing.

session_attributes_encoded.title: Session Attributes (Encoded)
session_attributes_encoded.description: A base64url-encoded JSON string containing session attributes collected from the user's browser. Provides additional attribution context if gclid, gbraid, or user identifiers are missing.

fields.label: Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)
fields.description: An alternative to the 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' field which can be used for Offline Conversions. If both 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' and 'Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)' are provided, the encoded field takes precedence.

session_attributes_key_value_pairs.title: Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)
session_attributes_key_value_pairs.description: An alternative to the 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' field which can be used for Offline Conversions. If both 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' and 'Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)' are provided, the encoded field takes precedence.

gad_source.title: GAD Source
gad_source.description: An aggregate parameter served in the URL to identify the source of traffic originating from ads. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16193746?sjid=2692215861659291994)

gad_campaignid.title: GAD Campaign ID
gad_campaignid.description: The ID of the specific ad campaign that drove the ad click. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16193746?sjid=2692215861659291994)

landing_page_url.title: Landing Page URL
landing_page_url.description: The full URL of the landing page on your website. This indicates the specific page the user first arrived on.

session_start_time_usec.title: Session Start Time
session_start_time_usec.description: The timestamp of when the user's session began on your website. This helps track the duration of user visits. The format should be a full ISO 8601 string. For example "2025-11-18T08:52:17.023Z".

landing_page_referrer.title: Landing Page Referrer
landing_page_referrer.description: The URL of the webpage that linked the user to your website. This helps understand the traffic sources leading to your site. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2382957?sjid=658827203196258052)

landing_page_user_agent.title: Landing Page User Agent
landing_page_user_agent.description: A string that identifies the user's browser and operating system. This information can be useful for understanding the technical environment of your users.

fields.label: Conversion Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

conversion_timestamp.title: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

fields.label: Email Address
fields.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

email_address.title: Email Address
email_address.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: Phone Number Country Code
fields.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

phone_country_code.title: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

fields.label: Phone Number
fields.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

phone_number.title: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: Order ID
fields.description: The order ID associated with the conversion. An order ID can only be used for one conversion per conversion action.

order_id.title: Order ID
order_id.description: The order ID associated with the conversion. An order ID can only be used for one conversion per conversion action.

fields.label: Value
fields.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

fields.label: Currency
fields.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

fields.label: Conversion Environment
fields.description: The environment this conversion was recorded on, e.g. APP or WEB. Sending the environment field requires an allowlist in your Google Ads account. Leave this field blank if your account has not been allowlisted.

conversion_environment.title: Conversion Environment
conversion_environment.description: The environment this conversion was recorded on, e.g. APP or WEB. Sending the environment field requires an allowlist in your Google Ads account. Leave this field blank if your account has not been allowlisted.

fields.label: Merchant Center ID
fields.description: The ID of the Merchant Center account where the items are uploaded.

merchant_id.title: Merchant Center ID
merchant_id.description: The ID of the Merchant Center account where the items are uploaded.

fields.label: Merchant Center Feed Country Code
fields.description: The ISO 3166 two-character region code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

merchant_country_code.title: Merchant Center Feed Country Code
merchant_country_code.description: The ISO 3166 two-character region code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

fields.label: Merchant Center Feed Language Code
fields.description: The ISO 639-1 language code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

merchant_language_code.title: Merchant Center Feed Language Code
merchant_language_code.description: The ISO 639-1 language code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

fields.label: Local Transaction Cost
fields.description: Sum of all transaction-level discounts, such as free shipping and coupon discounts for the whole cart.

local_cost.title: Local Transaction Cost
local_cost.description: Sum of all transaction-level discounts, such as free shipping and coupon discounts for the whole cart.

fields.label: Items
fields.description: Data of the items purchased.

product_id.title: Product ID
product_id.description: The ID of the item sold.

price.title: Price
price.description: Unit price excluding tax, shipping, and any transaction level discounts.

fields.label: Custom Variables
fields.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables.](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables)

ad_user_data_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad user data.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

actions.name: Conversion Adjustment V2
actions.description: Send a conversion adjustment to the Google Ads API.

fields.label: Conversion Action ID
fields.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

conversion_action.title: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

fields.label: Adjustment Type
fields.description: The adjustment type. See [Google’s documentation](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v11/ConversionAdjustmentTypeEnum.ConversionAdjustmentType) for details on each type.

adjustment_type.title: Adjustment Type
adjustment_type.description: The adjustment type. See [Google’s documentation](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v11/ConversionAdjustmentTypeEnum.ConversionAdjustmentType) for details on each type.

fields.label: Adjustment Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the adjustment occurred. Must be after the conversion timestamp. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". If no timestamp is provided, Segment will fall back on the current time.

adjustment_timestamp.title: Adjustment Timestamp
adjustment_timestamp.description: The date time at which the adjustment occurred. Must be after the conversion timestamp. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". If no timestamp is provided, Segment will fall back on the current time.

fields.label: Order ID
fields.description: The order ID of the conversion to be adjusted. If the conversion was reported with an order ID specified, that order ID must be used as the identifier here.

order_id.title: Order ID
order_id.description: The order ID of the conversion to be adjusted. If the conversion was reported with an order ID specified, that order ID must be used as the identifier here.

fields.label: GCLID
fields.description: Google click ID associated with the original conversion for this adjustment. This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

gclid.title: GCLID
gclid.description: Google click ID associated with the original conversion for this adjustment. This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

fields.label: Conversion Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the original conversion for this adjustment occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

conversion_timestamp.title: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the original conversion for this adjustment occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

fields.label: Restatement Value
fields.description: The restated conversion value. This is the value of the conversion after restatement. For example, to change the value of a conversion from 100 to 70, an adjusted value of 70 should be reported. Required for RESTATEMENT adjustments.

restatement_value.title: Restatement Value
restatement_value.description: The restated conversion value. This is the value of the conversion after restatement. For example, to change the value of a conversion from 100 to 70, an adjusted value of 70 should be reported. Required for RESTATEMENT adjustments.

fields.label: Restatement Currency Code
fields.description: The currency of the restated value. If not provided, then the default currency from the conversion action is used, and if that is not set then the account currency is used. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code, e.g. USD or EUR.

restatement_currency_code.title: Restatement Currency Code
restatement_currency_code.description: The currency of the restated value. If not provided, then the default currency from the conversion action is used, and if that is not set then the account currency is used. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code, e.g. USD or EUR.

fields.label: Phone Number Country Code
fields.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

phone_country_code.title: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

email_address.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

phone_number.title: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.description: First name of the user who performed the conversion.

first_name.title: First Name
first_name.description: First name of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: Last Name
fields.description: Last name of the user who performed the conversion. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

last_name.title: Last Name
last_name.description: Last name of the user who performed the conversion. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.description: City of the user who performed the conversion.

city.description: City of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: State
fields.description: State of the user who performed the conversion.

state.title: State
state.description: State of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: Country
fields.description: 2-letter country code in ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 of the user who performed the conversion.

country.title: Country
country.description: 2-letter country code in ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: Postal Code

postal_code.description: Postal code of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.description: Street address of the user who performed the conversion. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

street_address.title: Street Address
street_address.description: Street address of the user who performed the conversion. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: User Agent
fields.description: The user agent to enhance the original conversion. User agent can only be specified in enhancements with user identifiers. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device.

user_agent.title: User Agent
user_agent.description: The user agent to enhance the original conversion. User agent can only be specified in enhancements with user identifiers. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device.

fields.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

external_audience_id.title: External Audience ID

batch_size.description: The number of records to send in each batch.

fields.label: Event Name

event_name.title: Event Name
event_name.description: The name of the current Segment event.

fields.description: The ID of an existing Google list that you would like to sync users to. If you provide this, we will not create a new list.

fields.label: External ID Type
fields.description: Customer match upload key types.

fields.label: App ID
fields.description: A string that uniquely identifies a mobile application from which the data was collected. Required if external ID type is mobile advertising ID

app_id.title: App ID
app_id.description: A string that uniquely identifies a mobile application from which the data was collected. Required if external ID type is mobile advertising ID

fields.label: Connect to a Google Customer Match User List

id.title: ID

name.title: List Name

actions.name: Session Attributes Encoded Plugin
actions.description: Enriches Segment payloads with Session Attributes Encoded values from the page URL.
actions.defaultTrigger: type = "track" or type = "identify" or type = "page" or type = "group" or type = "alias"

actions.name: Upload Call Conversion
actions.description: Upload an offline call conversion to the Google Ads API.

fields.label: Conversion Action ID
fields.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

conversion_action.title: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

fields.label: Caller ID
fields.description: The caller ID from which this call was placed. Caller ID is expected to be in E.164 format with preceding + sign, e.g. "+16502531234".

caller_id.title: Caller ID
caller_id.description: The caller ID from which this call was placed. Caller ID is expected to be in E.164 format with preceding + sign, e.g. "+16502531234".

fields.label: Call Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the call occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

call_timestamp.title: Call Timestamp
call_timestamp.description: The date time at which the call occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

fields.label: Conversion Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

conversion_timestamp.title: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

fields.label: Value
fields.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

fields.label: Currency
fields.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

fields.label: Custom Variables
fields.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables).

custom_variables.title: Custom Variables
custom_variables.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables).

fields.label: Ad User Data Consent State
fields.description: This represents consent for ad user data. For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_user_data_consent_state.title: Ad User Data Consent State
ad_user_data_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad user data. For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

fields.label: Ad Personalization Consent State
fields.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_personalization_consent_state.title: Ad Personalization Consent State
ad_personalization_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

fields.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
fields.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

enable_batching.title: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

fields.label: Batch Size
fields.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

batch_size.title: Batch Size
batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

actions.name: Upload Click Conversion
actions.description: Upload an offline click conversion to the Google Ads API.

fields.label: Conversion Action ID
fields.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

conversion_action.title: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

fields.label: GCLID
fields.description: The Google click ID (gclid) associated with this conversion.

gclid.title: GCLID
gclid.description: The Google click ID (gclid) associated with this conversion.

fields.label: GBRAID
fields.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with app conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

gbraid.title: GBRAID
gbraid.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with app conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

fields.label: WBRAID
fields.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with web conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

wbraid.title: WBRAID
wbraid.description: The click identifier for clicks associated with web conversions and originating from iOS devices starting with iOS14.

fields.label: User IP Address
fields.description: The IP address of the user who initiated the conversion.

user_ip_address.title: User IP Address
user_ip_address.description: The IP address of the user who initiated the conversion.

fields.label: Session Attributes (Encoded)
fields.description: A base64url-encoded JSON string containing session attributes collected from the user's browser. Provides additional attribution context if gclid, gbraid, or user identifiers are missing.

session_attributes_encoded.title: Session Attributes (Encoded)
session_attributes_encoded.description: A base64url-encoded JSON string containing session attributes collected from the user's browser. Provides additional attribution context if gclid, gbraid, or user identifiers are missing.

fields.label: Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)
fields.description: An alternative to the 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' field which can be used for Offline Conversions. If both 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' and 'Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)' are provided, the encoded field takes precedence.

session_attributes_key_value_pairs.title: Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)
session_attributes_key_value_pairs.description: An alternative to the 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' field which can be used for Offline Conversions. If both 'Session Attributes (Encoded)' and 'Session Attributes (Key Value Pairs)' are provided, the encoded field takes precedence.

gad_source.title: GAD Source
gad_source.description: An aggregate parameter served in the URL to identify the source of traffic originating from ads. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16193746?sjid=2692215861659291994)

gad_campaignid.title: GAD Campaign ID
gad_campaignid.description: The ID of the specific ad campaign that drove the ad click. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/16193746?sjid=2692215861659291994)

landing_page_url.title: Landing Page URL
landing_page_url.description: The full URL of the landing page on your website. This indicates the specific page the user first arrived on.

session_start_time_usec.title: Session Start Time
session_start_time_usec.description: The timestamp of when the user's session began on your website. This helps track the duration of user visits. The format should be a full ISO 8601 string. For example "2025-11-18T08:52:17.023Z".

landing_page_referrer.title: Landing Page Referrer
landing_page_referrer.description: The URL of the webpage that linked the user to your website. This helps understand the traffic sources leading to your site. See [Google's docs](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/2382957?sjid=658827203196258052)

landing_page_user_agent.title: Landing Page User Agent
landing_page_user_agent.description: A string that identifies the user's browser and operating system. This information can be useful for understanding the technical environment of your users.

fields.label: Conversion Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

conversion_timestamp.title: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the conversion occurred. Must be after the click time. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00".

fields.label: Email Address
fields.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

email_address.title: Email Address
email_address.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: Phone Number Country Code
fields.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

phone_country_code.title: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

fields.label: Phone Number
fields.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

phone_number.title: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: Order ID
fields.description: The order ID associated with the conversion. An order ID can only be used for one conversion per conversion action.

order_id.title: Order ID
order_id.description: The order ID associated with the conversion. An order ID can only be used for one conversion per conversion action.

fields.label: Value
fields.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value of the conversion for the advertiser.

fields.label: Currency
fields.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

currency.title: Currency
currency.description: Currency associated with the conversion value. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code.

fields.label: Conversion Environment
fields.description: The environment this conversion was recorded on, e.g. APP or WEB. Sending the environment field requires an allowlist in your Google Ads account. Leave this field blank if your account has not been allowlisted.

conversion_environment.title: Conversion Environment
conversion_environment.description: The environment this conversion was recorded on, e.g. APP or WEB. Sending the environment field requires an allowlist in your Google Ads account. Leave this field blank if your account has not been allowlisted.

fields.label: Merchant Center ID
fields.description: The ID of the Merchant Center account where the items are uploaded.

merchant_id.title: Merchant Center ID
merchant_id.description: The ID of the Merchant Center account where the items are uploaded.

fields.label: Merchant Center Feed Country Code
fields.description: The ISO 3166 two-character region code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

merchant_country_code.title: Merchant Center Feed Country Code
merchant_country_code.description: The ISO 3166 two-character region code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

fields.label: Merchant Center Feed Language Code
fields.description: The ISO 639-1 language code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

merchant_language_code.title: Merchant Center Feed Language Code
merchant_language_code.description: The ISO 639-1 language code of the Merchant Center feed where the items are uploaded.

fields.label: Local Transaction Cost
fields.description: Sum of all transaction-level discounts, such as free shipping and coupon discounts for the whole cart.

local_cost.title: Local Transaction Cost
local_cost.description: Sum of all transaction-level discounts, such as free shipping and coupon discounts for the whole cart.

fields.label: Items
fields.description: Data of the items purchased.

product_id.title: Product ID
product_id.description: The ID of the item sold.

price.title: Price
price.description: Unit price excluding tax, shipping, and any transaction level discounts.

fields.label: Custom Variables
fields.description: The custom variables associated with this conversion. On the left-hand side, input the name of the custom variable as it appears in your Google Ads account. On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables.](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables)

On the right-hand side, map the Segment field that contains the corresponding value See [Google’s documentation on how to create custom conversion variables.](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/docs/conversions/conversion-custom-variables)

fields.description: This represents consent for ad user data.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_user_data_consent_state.title: Ad User Data Consent State
ad_user_data_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad user data.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

fields.label: Ad Personalization Consent State
fields.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

ad_personalization_consent_state.title: Ad Personalization Consent State
ad_personalization_consent_state.description: This represents consent for ad personalization. This can only be set for OfflineUserDataJobService and UserDataService.For more information on consent, refer to [Google Ads API Consent](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/rest/reference/rest/v21/Consent).

fields.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

actions.name: Upload Conversion Adjustment
actions.description: Upload a conversion adjustment to the Google Ads API.

fields.label: Conversion Action ID
fields.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

conversion_action.title: Conversion Action ID
conversion_action.description: The ID of the conversion action associated with this conversion.

fields.label: Adjustment Type
fields.description: The adjustment type. See [Google’s documentation](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v11/ConversionAdjustmentTypeEnum.ConversionAdjustmentType) for details on each type.

adjustment_type.title: Adjustment Type
adjustment_type.description: The adjustment type. See [Google’s documentation](https://developers.google.com/google-ads/api/reference/rpc/v11/ConversionAdjustmentTypeEnum.ConversionAdjustmentType) for details on each type.

fields.label: Adjustment Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the adjustment occurred. Must be after the conversion timestamp. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". If no timestamp is provided, Segment will fall back on the current time.

adjustment_timestamp.title: Adjustment Timestamp
adjustment_timestamp.description: The date time at which the adjustment occurred. Must be after the conversion timestamp. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". If no timestamp is provided, Segment will fall back on the current time.

fields.label: Order ID
fields.description: The order ID of the conversion to be adjusted. If the conversion was reported with an order ID specified, that order ID must be used as the identifier here.

order_id.title: Order ID
order_id.description: The order ID of the conversion to be adjusted. If the conversion was reported with an order ID specified, that order ID must be used as the identifier here.

fields.label: GCLID
fields.description: Google click ID associated with the original conversion for this adjustment. This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

gclid.title: GCLID
gclid.description: Google click ID associated with the original conversion for this adjustment. This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

fields.label: Conversion Timestamp
fields.description: The date time at which the original conversion for this adjustment occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

conversion_timestamp.title: Conversion Timestamp
conversion_timestamp.description: The date time at which the original conversion for this adjustment occurred. The timezone must be specified. The format is "yyyy-mm-dd hh:mm:ss+|-hh:mm", e.g. "2019-01-01 12:32:45-08:00". This is used for the GCLID Date Time Pair.

fields.label: Restatement Value
fields.description: The restated conversion value. This is the value of the conversion after restatement. For example, to change the value of a conversion from 100 to 70, an adjusted value of 70 should be reported. Required for RESTATEMENT adjustments.

restatement_value.title: Restatement Value
restatement_value.description: The restated conversion value. This is the value of the conversion after restatement. For example, to change the value of a conversion from 100 to 70, an adjusted value of 70 should be reported. Required for RESTATEMENT adjustments.

fields.label: Restatement Currency Code
fields.description: The currency of the restated value. If not provided, then the default currency from the conversion action is used, and if that is not set then the account currency is used. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code, e.g. USD or EUR.

restatement_currency_code.title: Restatement Currency Code
restatement_currency_code.description: The currency of the restated value. If not provided, then the default currency from the conversion action is used, and if that is not set then the account currency is used. This is the ISO 4217 3-character currency code, e.g. USD or EUR.

fields.label: Email Address
fields.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

email_address.title: Email Address
email_address.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: Phone Number Country Code
fields.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

phone_country_code.title: Phone Number Country Code
phone_country_code.description: The numeric country code to associate with the phone number. If not provided Segment will default to '+1'. If the country code does not start with '+' Segment will add it.

fields.label: Phone Number
fields.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

phone_number.title: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: First Name
fields.description: First name of the user who performed the conversion

first_name.title: First Name
first_name.description: First name of the user who performed the conversion

fields.label: Last Name
fields.description: Last name of the user who performed the conversion. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

last_name.title: Last Name
last_name.description: Last name of the user who performed the conversion. If not hashed, Segment will hash this value.

fields.label: City
fields.description: City of the user who performed the conversion.

city.title: City
city.description: City of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: State
fields.description: State of the user who performed the conversion.

state.title: State
state.description: State of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: Country
fields.description: 2-letter country code in ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 of the user who performed the conversion.

country.title: Country
country.description: 2-letter country code in ISO-3166-1 alpha-2 of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: Postal Code
fields.description: Postal code of the user who performed the conversion.

postal_code.title: Postal Code
postal_code.description: Postal code of the user who performed the conversion.

fields.label: Street Address
fields.description: Street address of the user who performed the conversion

street_address.title: Street Address
street_address.description: Street address of the user who performed the conversion

fields.label: User Agent
fields.description: The user agent to enhance the original conversion. User agent can only be specified in enhancements with user identifiers. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device.

user_agent.title: User Agent
user_agent.description: The user agent to enhance the original conversion. User agent can only be specified in enhancements with user identifiers. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device.

fields.label: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
fields.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

enable_batching.title: Batch Data to Google Enhanced Conversions
enable_batching.description: If true, Segment will batch events before sending to Google’s APIs. Google accepts batches of up to 2000 events.

fields.label: Batch Size

batch_size.description: Maximum number of events to include in each batch.

actions.name: Upload Enhanced Conversion (Legacy)
actions.description: Upload a conversion enhancement to the legacy Google Enhanced Conversions API.

fields.label: Conversion Label
fields.description: The Google Ads conversion label. You can find it in your Google Ads account using the instructions in the article [Google Ads conversions](https://support.google.com/tagmanager/answer/6105160?hl=en).

conversion_label.title: Conversion Label
conversion_label.description: The Google Ads conversion label. You can find it in your Google Ads account using the instructions in the article [Google Ads conversions](https://support.google.com/tagmanager/answer/6105160?hl=en).

fields.label: Email
fields.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

email.title: Email
email.description: Email address of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

fields.label: Order ID
fields.description: Order ID or Transaction ID of the conversion event. Google requires an Order ID even if the event is not an ecommerce event. Learn more in the article [Use a transaction ID to minimize duplicate conversions](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/6386790?hl=en&ref_topic=3165803).

transaction_id.title: Order ID
transaction_id.description: Order ID or Transaction ID of the conversion event. Google requires an Order ID even if the event is not an ecommerce event. Learn more in the article [Use a transaction ID to minimize duplicate conversions](https://support.google.com/google-ads/answer/6386790?hl=en&ref_topic=3165803).

fields.label: User Agent
fields.description: User agent of the individual who triggered the conversion event. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device. This field is optional but recommended.

user_agent.title: User Agent
user_agent.description: User agent of the individual who triggered the conversion event. This should match the user agent of the request that sent the original conversion so the conversion and its enhancement are either both attributed as same-device or both attributed as cross-device. This field is optional but recommended.

fields.label: Conversion Time
fields.description: Timestamp of the conversion event.

fields.label: Value
fields.description: The monetary value attributed to the conversion event.

value.title: Value
value.description: The monetary value attributed to the conversion event.

fields.label: Currency Code
fields.description: Currency of the purchase or items associated with the conversion event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format.

currency_code.title: Currency Code
currency_code.description: Currency of the purchase or items associated with the conversion event, in 3-letter ISO 4217 format.

fields.label: App Conversion for Incrementality Study
fields.description: Set to true if this is an app conversion for an incrementality study.

is_app_incrementality.title: App Conversion for Incrementality Study
is_app_incrementality.description: Set to true if this is an app conversion for an incrementality study.

fields.label: PCC Game Flag
fields.description: Alpha feature offered by Google for gaming industry. When set to true, Segment will send pcc_game = 1 to Google.

pcc_game.title: PCC Game Flag
pcc_game.description: Alpha feature offered by Google for gaming industry. When set to true, Segment will send pcc_game = 1 to Google.

fields.label: Phone Number
fields.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000.

phone_number.title: Phone Number
phone_number.description: Phone number of the individual who triggered the conversion event, in E.164 standard format, e.g. +14150000000.

fields.label: First Name
fields.description: First name of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

first_name.title: First Name
first_name.description: First name of the individual who triggered the conversion event.

fields.label: Last Name
fields.description: Last name of the individual who triggered the conversion event.
